description_long: This is a tall, ornate stone obelisk mounted on a multi-tiered pedestal, likely made of weathered marble or granite. The base is broad and stepped, with decorative molding and corner columns, giving it a monumental appearance. The shaft rises vertically and is topped with a sculpted figure or draped form, adding a classical or memorial character. The surface shows signs of aging, with discoloration and some erosion, suggesting it has been exposed to the elements for a long period. The front face features a carved relief or emblem, possibly a wreath or shield, indicating commemorative or historical significance. The overall design is elaborate, with detailed ornamentation on both the base and the upper section.
description: A tall, ornate stone obelisk with a sculpted figure on top and a multi-tiered, decorated base, showing signs of weathering.
description_view_0: Front view showing the sculpted figure and a prominent relief or emblem on the upper section above the pedestal.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the draped back of the figure and the detailed corner columns of the pedestal.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the back of the draped figure and the stepped, solid structure of the base.
description_view_3: Angled rear-side view showing the side profile of the figure and the layered, weathered base.
attribute_ornamentation: Decorative relief or emblem on the front upper section, sculpted draped figure on top, and ornamental corner columns on the pedestal.
attribute_base_design: The base is broad, stepped, and rectangular with multiple tiers, featuring decorative molding and corner columns, all made from the same stone material.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is adorned with a sculpted, draped figure, possibly representing a person or symbolic form, with detailed folds and classical styling.
